title: Roleplay First, Then Roll
shortVersion: Say what your character tries to do before asking for a roll.
fullExplanation: Players describe the action in plain language. The DM decides whether it works, requires a roll, or has a consequence.
whyTheTableUsesIt: It keeps the focus on the shared fiction instead of turning every moment into a button on a sheet.
exampleInPlay: Say "I want to talk the guard into letting us through" instead of "I roll Persuasion."
exceptions: Fast repeated checks may be abbreviated once everyone understands the scene.
